nfpa.label: NFPA 70E 2024
nfpa.description: Uses the National Fire Protection Association NFPA 70E Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace (2024 edition). Terminology reflects the current national consensus standard used by OSHA and industry.

ppe_categories.description: Arc-rated clothing (min. 4 cal/cm² rating), face shield, heavy-duty leather gloves
Arc-rated clothing (min. 8 cal/cm² rating), arc-rated face shield or arc flash suit hood
Arc-rated clothing system (min. 25 cal/cm² rating), arc flash suit hood
Arc-rated clothing system (min. 40 cal/cm² rating), arc flash suit hood
Arc Flash PPE Category method cannot be used — incident energy analysis required. Work above available PPE arc rating is prohibited.

boundaries.name: Limited Approach Boundary
boundaries.definition: Approach limit within which a shock hazard exists. Unqualified persons may not cross without continuous escort by a qualified person.

boundaries.name: Restricted Approach Boundary
boundaries.definition: Increased likelihood of electric shock due to arc-over combined with inadvertent movement. Qualified persons only, with energized electrical work permit and appropriate PPE.

boundaries.name: Arc Flash Boundary
boundaries.definition: Distance at which incident energy equals 1.2 cal/cm² (5 J/cm²) — onset of a second-degree burn on unprotected skin. All body parts inside this boundary must be protected.

nfpa.boundary_note: Note: The 'Prohibited Approach Boundary' was removed from NFPA 70E in 2012. The current standard uses three boundaries only.

loto_steps.title: Identify All Energy Sources
loto_steps.detail: Determine all possible sources of electrical supply using applicable up-to-date drawings, diagrams, and identification tags. (NFPA 70E 120.5)

loto_steps.title: Interrupt the Load & Open Disconnecting Devices
loto_steps.detail: After properly interrupting load current, open the disconnecting device(s) for each source. Where possible, visually verify all blades are fully open. (NFPA 70E 120.5)

loto_steps.title: Release Stored Electrical Energy
loto_steps.detail: Discharge capacitors, de-energize all stored electrical energy sources. (NFPA 70E 120.5)

loto_steps.title: Release or Block Stored Mechanical Energy
loto_steps.detail: Block elevated parts, release spring tension, bleed pneumatic and hydraulic lines. (NFPA 70E 120.5)

loto_steps.title: Apply Lockout/Tagout Devices
loto_steps.detail: Apply lockout/tagout devices in accordance with a documented and established procedure. (NFPA 70E 120.4)

loto_steps.title: Verify De-energization
loto_steps.detail: Use an adequately rated portable test instrument to test each phase conductor or circuit part — phase-to-phase AND phase-to-ground. Verify test instrument operation before and after each test. (NFPA 70E 120.5)

loto_steps.title: Perform Work
loto_steps.detail: Electrically safe work condition established. Work may proceed. Retest if job location is left unattended. (NFPA 70E 120.5)

complex_loto_model.name: Simple vs. Complex Lockout
complex_loto_model.description: NFPA 70E distinguishes between Simple and Complex lockout/tagout situations.

levels.description: One qualified person de-energizes one set of conductors or circuit parts from a single energy source — no written plan required
Multiple energy sources, multiple crews, multiple crafts, multiple locations, complex switching sequences, or job spans more than one work period — written plan of execution required

nfpa.qualified_person_def: One who has the skills and knowledge related to the construction and operation of electrical equipment and installations, and has received safety training to recognize and avoid the hazards involved.
nfpa.lock_spec: Lockout devices shall be substantial enough to prevent removal without excessive force. Tagout devices shall warn against energizing equipment.
nfpa.arc_flash_review: At intervals not to exceed 5 years
nfpa.training_interval: At intervals not to exceed 3 years
nfpa.two_methods_note: PPE selection uses either: (1) Incident Energy Analysis Method (130.5(G)) OR (2) Arc Flash PPE Category Method (130.7(C)(15)). Both methods shall NOT be used on the same piece of equipment.

workplace.label: Workplace Standards
workplace.description: Uses enhanced workplace safety procedures including facility-specific LOTO protocols, hazardous energy control, and PPE requirements aligned with industrial facility standards.

ppe_categories.description: Arc-rated shirt and pants or coverall
Arc-rated shirt and pants, arc-rated coverall, face shield
Full arc flash suit, maximum protection
Energized work strictly prohibited — equipment must be de-energized

boundaries.name: Limited Approach Boundary
boundaries.definition: Closest unqualified persons may approach without escort from a qualified person.

boundaries.name: Restricted Approach Boundary
boundaries.definition: Qualified persons only. Treated same as direct contact with energized conductor.

boundaries.name: Prohibited Approach Boundary
boundaries.definition: Extremely close proximity — same protection required as direct contact. Energized work permit required.

boundaries.name: Arc Flash Protection Boundary
boundaries.definition: Distance at which incident energy equals 1.2 cal/cm² — onset of second-degree burn. Arc-rated PPE required inside this boundary.

loto_steps.title: Identify All Energy Sources
loto_steps.detail: Identify all electrical, mechanical, pneumatic, hydraulic, chemical, and thermal energy sources using applicable drawings and HECPs.

loto_steps.title: Notify Affected Employees
loto_steps.detail: Notify all affected employees that equipment is being taken out of service and the reason for the lockout.

loto_steps.title: Shut Down Equipment
loto_steps.detail: Shut down equipment using the normal stopping procedure.

loto_steps.title: Isolate All Energy Sources
loto_steps.detail: Open all disconnecting devices for each energy source. Visually verify all blades are fully open where possible.

loto_steps.title: Apply Lockout/Tagout Devices
loto_steps.detail: Apply red lockout locks (Master Lock or American Lock only). One key per associate — never shared. Apply laminated tags with name and date.

loto_steps.title: Release or Restrain Stored Energy
loto_steps.detail: Bleed down pneumatic lines, block mechanical parts, discharge capacitors, release spring tension, and neutralize all stored energy.

loto_steps.title: Verify Isolation (Tryout)
loto_steps.detail: Attempt to start equipment using normal start controls to verify it cannot be re-energized. Test with an adequately rated test instrument phase-to-phase and phase-to-ground.

loto_steps.title: Perform Work
loto_steps.detail: Work may now proceed. Maintain locks throughout. Do not remove another person's lock.

complex_loto_model.name: Facility Service Model
complex_loto_model.description: Four service levels define the extent of LOTOTO required based on maintenance complexity.

levels.description: Basic operations with Category 3 interlocks — no LOTOTO required
Full HECP Section D compliance — comprehensive maintenance

workplace.qualified_person_def: An associate approved by the Electrical Board to perform electrical power distribution work. Must complete required training and be listed on the departmental authorization list.
workplace.lock_spec: Approved safety lockout locks — RED color required. One key per associate, never shared.
workplace.qualified_person_definition: An employee trained and authorized to perform electrical work under workplace safety standards, including arc flash hazard analysis and LOTO procedures.
